div1.entryFree: Seel, to close up the eyes, to blind (originally a term of falconry): come, --ing night, scarf up the tender eye of pitiful day, Mcb. III, 2, 46. when light-winged toys of feathered Cupid s. with wanton dullness my speculative and officed instruments, Oth. I, 3, 270 (Qq foyles). to s. her father's eyes up close as oak, III, 3, 210 (Qq seal).* the wise gods s. our eyes, Ant. III, 13, 112. cf. Seal.
